pattern: The range of a subarray is its max minus its min, so the total splits cleanly into (sum of subarray maximums) minus (sum of subarray minimums) -- each solvable independently with a monotonic stack that counts, for every element, how many subarrays it is the extreme of.
hints: Range(subarray) = max(subarray) - min(subarray), so the whole sum splits into two independent sums: total max minus total min over all subarrays.
For a fixed element, count how many subarrays it is the maximum (or minimum) of, using how far the same value or a bigger/smaller one is on each side.
A monotonic stack gives, for each index, the distance to the previous strictly-greater and next greater-or-equal element (mirror it for minimums) in one linear pass each.

edge_inputs.description: single element, range of every subarray is 0
all equal values, every range is 0
duplicate maxima and minima on both sides of a valley
extreme constraint values at both ends

stress.description: random values across the full constraint range at max length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    nums = [rng.randint(-1000000000, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

stress.description: many duplicate values, stresses the tie-breaking in the monotonic stacks
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    pool = [rng.randint(-5, 5) for _ in range(20)]
    nums = [rng.choice(pool) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
